items.answer_rationale: Black’s forcing rook check drives the white king up the board. The rook continues checking from successive ranks, while the black king and bishop protect the checking squares. White’s king cannot escape the checking net, and the sequence repeats by ...Rf8+, yielding a forced perpetual check (and therefore a draw) rather than allowing White time to take the bishop.
